mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, signals an environment where the Federal Reserve will maintain or increase the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS), leading to higher discount rates and upward pressure on long-term Treasury yields over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields consistently decline over 20-day periods when CPIAUCSL remains above 5% year-over-year and FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A significant tightening in the labor market, indicated by the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falling below a critical threshold, suggests increasing wage pressures and robust economic activity, which drives inflation expectations and pushes 10-year Treasury yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is 4.7% as of t-1mo, showing potential for further tightening
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields fail to show a statistically significant increase over 20-day periods when UNRATE is consistently below 4.0% for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Robust consumer spending, reflected in strong retail sales (RSAFS), combined with expanding industrial production (INDPRO), indicates broad economic strength. This strong demand and supply-side activity fuels higher growth and inflation expectations, leading to rising 10-year Treasury yields over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND INDPRO 3-month average growth > 0.3%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline or remain flat for more than 60 trading days despite the trigger condition being met for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: During an aggressive monetary tightening cycle, characterized by rapid increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS), markets anticipate a future economic slowdown or recession. This expectation drives investors into safe-haven assets, pushing long-term Treasury yields lower over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.75% in the last 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS has been rising in recent months
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields continue to rise for more than 60 trading days after the trigger condition is met, in at least 70% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening investment and consumer confidence in the economy's future prospects. This reduction in future growth expectations translates into lower long-term interest rates and thus downward pressure on 10-year Treasury yields over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields consistently rise over 5-day periods when HOUST shows a clear and persistent downward trend for at least 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A severe deterioration in the labor market, evidenced by a significant rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) coupled with declining non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), signals an impending or ongoing recession. This drives safe-haven demand for Treasuries, pushing yields lower over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields increase or remain stable during periods where both UNRATE is rising significantly and PAYEMS is falling for at least two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) above its long-term trend indicates robust economic expansion. This strong growth environment leads to higher inflation expectations and increased demand for capital, which collectively push 10-year Treasury yields higher over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields consistently decline or remain flat when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0% year-over-year for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent strength in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust supply-side capacity and underlying demand, which supports higher long-term growth expectations. This positive outlook for economic activity translates into upward pressure on 10-year Treasury yields over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth shows a negative correlation with 10-year Treasury yields over any 5-year rolling window, with a correlation coefficient below -0.2.
